title_en: Nigel Farage’s stunts made him a successful politician. They won’t make him PM
title_zh: 奈杰尔·法拉奇的“特技秀”让他成了成功政客，但未必能让他当上首相
rubric_en: The theatrics could point to Reform UK’s weaknesses
rubric_zh: 这种戏剧性表演也可能暴露了改革党（Reform UK）的短板

paragraphs.en: In Britain, you can pay £500 ($670) to stand next to the prime minister on live TV. A humbling election-night tradition sees politicians line up on stage with fellow candidates as the votes in their constituency are read out. Plenty of publicity-seekers think the election deposit is a fair price to pay for a moment in the spotlight. In 2024 Sir Keir Starmer—for now still the prime minister—gave his acceptance speech flanked by a man dressed as Elmo, a furry red children’s-TV character.
paragraphs.zh: 在英国，你可以花 500 英镑（约 670 美元）来在电视直播里与首相并肩站在舞台上。竞选夜的这一传统颇具“见人就俯首”的意味：政客们排队上台，与其所在选区的对手在得票结果宣布时同框出镜。许多爱出风头的人认为，竞选押金是换取一小段聚光灯时刻的“公平价格”。2024 年，时任首相的基尔·斯塔默（Keir Starmer）在发表胜选演说时，身边就站着一位红色毛绒装的儿童电视角色 Elmo（艾尔摩）扮相的人。

paragraphs.en: Later this year Nigel Farage, the leader of the populist-right Reform UK party, will be treated to a particularly surreal election-night spectacle. No candidates for the major parties will stand against him in the by-election he triggered with great fanfare on July 7th, by resigning as MP for Clacton in a televised address (he hopes to be re-elected). Instead, the man who aspires to become prime minister at the next general election will be surrounded by satirists and loonies. Count Binface, whose real name is Jonathan Harvey, has already emerged as a leading challenger.
paragraphs.zh: 今年晚些时候，民粹右翼改革党（Reform UK）领袖奈杰尔·法拉奇（Nigel Farage）将迎来一场尤其离奇的竞选之夜场景。7 月 7 日，他在电视讲话中宣布辞去克拉克顿（Clacton）议员（MP）职位，因而触发了补选，并以极高调的方式安排了这一结果——他希望自己能再次当选，因此也就是这场补选的“亮相”对象。届时，渴望在下届大选中成为首相的他身边，不会有主要政党的候选人与他对阵；取而代之的将是讽刺喜剧演员和“怪才们”。杰里米·哈维（Jonathan Harvey）本名的表演艺人 Count Binface 现在已经成为主要挑战者。

paragraphs.en: The farce is befitting of the by-election. Mr Farage called it to set the cat among the pigeons while he faces scrutiny of his finances, including an investigation by Parliament’s standards commissioner. The body is reviewing a £5m gift made to him by cryptocurrency billionaire Christopher Harborne. Mr Farage told viewers the by-election would be the “people versus the establishment”. In reality, it is a squalid attempt to have the residents of Reform’s safest constituency clear him of wrongdoing. Now it looks as though the joke might be on the rabble-rousing populist.
paragraphs.zh: 这种荒诞情境正配得上这场补选。法拉奇之所以这样做，是想在自己面对财务审查时搅起一阵骚动，其中包括议会标准委员会对其展开的调查。该委员会正在审查一笔由加密货币巨头克里斯托弗·哈博恩（Christopher Harborne）赠予他的 500 万英镑捐款。法拉奇对观众称，这场补选将是“人民与建制派”的对决。实际上，这更像是一场龌龊的尝试：想借改革党最安全的选区选民之力，让自己洗脱不当行为嫌疑。如今看起来，开这个玩笑的人反而可能是爱煽动民粹情绪的法拉奇本人。

paragraphs.en: The misstep is revealing. Mr Farage has built his career on political mischief-making. His stunts as a member of the European Parliament caught the attention of British voters in the 2000s. In the 2010s, they made him a household name, culminating in the Brexit referendum in 2016. His dramatic returns to politics in 2019 and 2024 allowed his new party, the Brexit Party (now Reform), to give the Conservatives a bloody nose. In these moments, he dominated British politics.
paragraphs.zh: 这次失手颇具启示性。法拉奇多年来的政治生涯建立在制造政治乱子的基础上。2000 年代，他在欧洲议会任职期间的一连串闹剧吸引了英国选民的注意。到 2010 年代，这些做法使他一跃成为家喻户晓的人物，最终在 2016 年的“脱欧”公投中达到顶峰。2019 年和 2024 年他戏剧性地重返政坛，促成其新党“脱欧党”（后更名为改革党）在当时对保守党造成了“迎头痛击”。在那些时刻，他曾一度主导着英国政治。

paragraphs.en: The challenge facing Reform now is different. It has led opinion polls for more than a year and must transform from a loud insurgency into a credible alternative government—a tall order for a party with just eight MPs. After setting an artificial deadline of May 2026 for accepting defectors, fewer crossed the floor than Mr Farage might have hoped. Two, Robert Jenrick and Suella Braverman, were given official spokesperson roles, for the treasury and education, along with the party’s deputy leader, Richard Tice (business, trade and energy), and its former chairman, Zia Yusuf (home affairs). But the party still has no spokespeople on issues including defence, foreign policy, health and welfare.
paragraphs.zh: 改革党如今面临的挑战完全不同。该党已在民意调查中领先一年多，但必须从一个声势浩大的“叛乱性”势力，转变为可信的替代政府。对于只有八名议员的小党来说，这是一项艰巨任务。该党曾在2026年5月设下一个人为的“引蛇入洞”截止期限，结果真正改投党籍的人数仍远少于法拉奇所希望的。共有两人，罗伯特·詹里克（Robert Jenrick）和苏拉·布雷弗曼（Suella Braverman），被授予正式发言人职务，分别负责财政和教育工作，加上党副主席理查德·蒂斯（Richard Tice）负责商业、贸易和能源事务，以及前主席齐娅·优素福（Zia Yusuf）负责内政。但该党在国防、外交、医疗和福利等问题上仍然没有发言人。

paragraphs.en: Neither does it seem as if the party is substantiating its policy platform. Though Mr Jenrick has rowed back on Reform’s previous profligate tax-and-spend plans, the party has adopted unworkable proposals for mass deportations. It has offered little detail on policy for the National Health Service or Britain’s armed forces. The public seems to have noticed. According to Ipsos, a polling firm, the share of Britons who say Reform is “ready to form the next government” has fallen from 29% in September 2025 to 21% in June 2026 (see chart 1).
paragraphs.zh: 同样地，该党也似乎并未真正把其政策框架落到实处。尽管詹里克已经撤回了改革党先前那些大开支、偏向高税负与高支出的计划，该党却采纳了不可行的大规模遣返提案。它几乎没有就英国国家医疗服务体系（NHS）或英国武装部队的政策提供任何细节。公众显然已经注意到了这一点。根据民调公司Ipsos的数据显示，表示“改革党已经准备好组建下一届政府”的英国人比例，从2025年9月的29%降至2026年6月的21%（见图1）。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, Reform’s political space has shrunk. In July 2025 around 30% of people had a favourable view of Mr Farage, according to YouGov, another pollster. That was far higher than of his rivals, Sir Keir and Kemi Badenoch, the leader of the Conservatives. Since then Mr Farage’s rating has fallen to 25%; Ms Badenoch has experienced a surge, to 31%; and Labour has all but replaced Sir Keir with his presumed successor Andy Burnham, who is viewed favourably by 34% (see chart 2). Restore Britain, a new far-right party, has siphoned some voters from Reform’s right. Meanwhile the share of Britons saying immigration—the party’s raison d’être—is their top issue has fallen, perhaps owing to declining migration rates and a slight slowdown in small-boat arrivals across the channel.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，改革党的政治空间在缩小。根据另一家民调机构YouGov的数据，2025年7月左右，大约30%的受访者对法拉奇持有好感，这一比例远高于他的对手——凯尔（Sir Keir）和当时的保守党领袖凯米·巴德诺克（Kemi Badenoch）。此后法拉奇的好感度降至25%；巴德诺克则大幅上升至31%；而工党几乎已由凯尔让位，现任其潜在接班人安迪·伯纳姆（Andy Burnham）的好感度为34%（见图2）。新成立的极右政党“重建英国”（Restore Britain）则从改革党右翼吸走了部分选民。与此同时，认为移民是自己首要问题的英国人比例也下降了，这或许与移民率下降、以及英吉利海峡“橡皮艇/小船”入境速度放缓有关。

paragraphs.en: That is not to say Reform has hit a ceiling. Plenty of voters hold views similar to Mr Farage’s and are dissatisfied with Labour and the Conservatives. His party continues to lead opinion polls and did well in the local elections in May. The political conditions for the populist right could improve, not least if Mr Burnham turns out to be a dud. But Mr Farage’s party has not expanded its appeal in the past year.
paragraphs.zh: 这并不意味着改革党已经触顶。仍有大量选民的观点与法拉奇相近，并对工党与保守党感到失望。该党继续在民意调查中名列前茅，并在2025年5月的地方选举中表现良好。对于民粹右翼来说，政治环境仍可能继续改善，尤其是如果伯纳姆最终证实不具领导力的话。但在过去一年里，法拉奇的政党并未扩大其吸引力。

paragraphs.en: Amid these challenges, a by-election in Clacton is not so much a show of defiance—as Mr Farage would portray it—as a retreat to his comfort zone. The populist leader would rather be pouring pints for a photo-op than poring over policy proposals. Playing ringmaster for the media circus comes more naturally than marshalling prospective ministers. There is every chance that Mr Farage wins Clacton (barring an unthinkable victory for Count Binface) while dashing his chances of becoming prime minister at the same time.■
paragraphs.zh: 在这些挑战中，在克拉克顿举行的补选更像是一种退回到自己舒适区的举动，而不是法拉奇先生所说的“反叛”表态。这个民粹主义领袖宁愿在镜头前端着啤酒杯拍照，也不愿埋头研究政策提案。扮演媒体马戏团的“马戏团主持人”比起协调潜在部长的组成更顺手。几乎可以肯定的是，法拉奇先生很可能会赢得克拉克顿（除非出现对本·斐特斯（Count Binface）不可想象的胜利），同时也会大幅削弱他成为英国首相的机会。

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如想获取对英国最重磅新闻的更深入专家解读，请订阅 Blighty，我们每周推出的订阅制通讯。
